steps.title: Trace the artist description
steps.content: The text first introduces Kenji Morita as an artist known for waterfall paintings. It then describes recurring traits of those paintings: large size, cropped views of waterfalls, and recognizable Japanese techniques and materials.

steps.title: Reject absent comparisons
steps.content: Choice B discusses a technique and multiple artists, which the text does not do. Choice C invents a comparison between paintings. Choice D says Morita is overlooked, contradicting known worldwide. The remaining choice matches the passage's structure. The answer is A.
